I need to review whether pace ₢AlAAB (private-pool-always-burn-default-bridge) is complete. Check the current state of these files against the acceptance criteria listed below.

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. No default-pool code path remains in stitch or depot constants
2. `RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL` is required (regime validator rejects empty)
3. `RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE` default is `e2-standard-2`, validated as CE type
4. Stitch emits only `pool.name`, never `machineType` in options
5. depot_create creates pool using RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE, depot_destroy deletes pool
6. Decision chain documented in spec (not just paddock)
7. Build passes

**Files to check:**
- Tools/rbw/rbf_Foundry.sh — stitch function, look for any remaining default-pool conditional or machineType in options
- Tools/rbw/rbgp_Payor.sh — depot_create pool creation, depot_destroy pool deletion
- Tools/rbw/rbgc_Constants.sh — RBGC_WORKER_POOL_ID and API endpoint
- Tools/rbw/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh — should have NO default-pool quota path
- Tools/rbw/rbrr_regime.sh — RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL required (1-512), RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE validated as CE type
- .rbk/rbrr.env — RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE default should be e2-standard-2
- Also check Tools/rbw/rbrr.validator.sh if it exists

For each file, read it and report:
- What the current state is regarding the acceptance criteria
- Any remaining default-pool code paths
- Any issues or gaps

Be thorough - read each file completely.

   133→  buv_lock RBRR

     2→{rbtgo_depot_create}
     3→
     4→NOTE: This operation creates an
     5→{rbtgi_depot}
     6→- a cohesive unit of Recipe Bottle infrastructure including the depot project, build bucket, container repository, Mason service account, and
     7→{rbtgi_cbv2_connection}
     8→that share their productive lifecycle.
     9→
    10→The
    11→{rbtr_payor}
    12→creates a new depot infrastructure from the
    13→{rbtgi_payor_project}:
    14→
    15→//axhop_parameter_from_type
    16→{rbtgi_depot}
    17→. *Depot Name*:
    18→.. {rbbc_require}
    19→`«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»` provided as first argument
    20→.. Validate depot name: lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens only, max 20 chars
    21→
    22→//axhos_step
    23→. *Validate Rubric Repo URL (GitLab required)*:
    24→.. {rbbc_require}
    25→{rbrr_rubric_repo_url}
    26→is set in regime (`RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL` in `rbrr.env`) or {rbbc_show} tabtarget hint for `rbw-Pgl` then {rbbc_fatal} "Cannot proceed without GitLab rubric repo"
    27→.. {rbbc_require}
    28→URL begins with `https://gitlab.com/` or {rbbc_show} tabtarget hint for `rbw-Pgl` (GitLab setup guide) then {rbbc_fatal} "Cannot proceed without GitLab rubric repo"
    29→
    30→//axhop_parameter_from_stdin
    31→. *GitLab Project Access Token (stdin)*:
    32→.. {rbbc_show}
    33→rubric repo URL
    34→.. Read one line from stdin: `«GITLAB_TOKEN»`
    35→.. If empty, {rbbc_show} tabtarget hint for `rbw-Pgl` (GitLab setup guide) then {rbbc_fatal} "Token is empty"
    36→
    37→//axhos_step
    38→. *Validate Rubric Repo URL reachability*:
    39→.. Construct authenticated URL using bash builtin: `https://oauth2:«GITLAB_TOKEN»@` + URL path from
    40→{rbrr_rubric_repo_url}
    41→.. {rbbc_call}
    42→`git ls-remote «AUTHENTICATED_URL» HEAD` to verify reachability and credential validity, stderr captured to temp file
    43→.. {rbbc_require}
    44→successful exit or {rbbc_fatal} "Rubric repo URL unreachable or token invalid — see «stderr_file»"
    45→.. Discard `«AUTHENTICATED_URL»` from memory (token must not persist)
    46→
    47→//axhos_step
    48→. *Authenticate as
    49→{rbtr_payor}*:
    50→.. Execute
    51→{rbtoe_payor_authenticate}
    52→
    53→//axhos_step
    54→. *Generate Depot Project ID*:
    55→.. {rbbc_store}
    56→`«TIMESTAMP»` as current time in YYYYMMDDHHmm format
    57→.. Generate project ID: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-«TIMESTAMP»`
    58→.. {rbbc_store}
    59→as `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    60→.. {rbbc_require}
    61→total length ≤ 30 characters

    66→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/create[projects.create] with:
    67→... projectId: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    68→... displayName: `RB Depot: «INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»`
    69→... parent: `{rbrp_parent_type}s/{rbrp_parent_id}` (if parent_type != `none`)
    70→.. {rbbc_await}
    71→returned operation

    73→project creation successful

    78→link:https://cloud.google.com/billing/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects/updateBillingInfo[cloudbilling.projects.updateBillingInfo] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`

    81→HTTP 200 response confirming billing linkage

   107→... workerPoolId: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-pool`

   143→link:https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/insert[storage.buckets.insert] with:
   144→... name: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-bucket`
   145→... location: `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
   146→... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
   147→... lifecycle rule: delete objects after 1 day

   153→.. Construct repository name: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-repository`

   155→link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/create[artifactregistry.repositories.create] with:
   156→... repositoryId: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-repository`
   157→... format: `DOCKER`
   158→... location: `{rbrr_gcp_region}`
   159→... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}`
   160→.. {rbbc_await}
   161→returned operation
   162→.. {rbbc_store}
   163→`«GAR_REPOSITORY»` as created repository name
   164→
   165→//axhos_step
   166→. *Create
   167→{rbtr_mason}
   168→Service Account*:
   169→.. NOTE: The
   170→{rbtr_mason}
   171→replaces Cloud Build's implicit service account to provide deterministic permissions and predictable authorization within the depot infrastructure.
   172→.. {rbbc_call}
   173→link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
   174→... accountId: `mason-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»`
   175→... displayName: `Mason for RB Depot: «INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»`
   176→... project: `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
   177→.. {rbbc_store}
   178→`«MASON_EMAIL»` from response `.email` field
   179→
   180→//axhos_step
   181→. *Configure
   182→{rbtr_mason}
   183→Permissions*:
   184→.. {rbbc_call}
   185→link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories/setIamPolicy[repositories.setIamPolicy] for `«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
   186→... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/artifactregistry.writer`
   187→.. {rbbc_call}
   188→link:https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/setIamPolicy[buckets.setIamPolicy] for `«BUILD_BUCKET»`
   189→... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/storage.objectViewer`
   190→.. {rbbc_call}
   191→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/setIamPolicy[projects.setIamPolicy] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
   192→... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/viewer`
   193→... Add `serviceAccount:«MASON_EMAIL»` with `roles/logging.logWriter`
   194→
   195→//axhos_step
   196→. *Enable Cloud Build service agent to impersonate
   197→{rbtr_mason}*:
   198→.. {rbbc_call}
   199→link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/setIamPolicy[serviceAccounts.setIamPolicy] for `«MASON_EMAIL»`
   200→.. Add member: `serviceAccount:service-«PROJECT_NUMBER»@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
   201→.. Grant role: `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator`
   202→.. NOTE: Cloud Build service agent is Google-provisioned (auto-created when Cloud Build API is enabled), not a user-created service account. The `tokenCreator` role enables build steps running as {rbtr_mason} (via the trigger's `serviceAccount` field) to generate fresh access tokens for GCP API calls — required by the OCI Layout Bridge pattern for GAR authentication (docker login, crane push). This is distinct from `serviceAccountUser` (actAs), which the {rbtr_director} holds on {rbtr_mason} for submitting builds.
   203→.. {rbbc_require}
   204→IAM binding successful
   205→
   206→//axhos_step
   207→. *Store GitLab credentials in Secret Manager (3 secrets)*:
   208→.. Generate `«WEBHOOK_SECRET»` as random UUID via `uuidgen` (output to temp file, read with bash builtin)
   209→.. Base64-encode `«GITLAB_TOKEN»` (used for both api and read_api secrets)
   210→.. Base64-encode `«WEBHOOK_SECRET»`
   211→.. For each secret (`RBGC_CBV2_API_TOKEN_SECRET_NAME`, `RBGC_CBV2_READ_TOKEN_SECRET_NAME`, `RBGC_CBV2_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME`):
   212→... {rbbc_call}
   213→link:https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.secrets/create[secrets.create] with:
   214→.... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
   215→.... secretId: secret name constant
   216→.... body: `{"replication": {"automatic": {}}}`
   217→... {rbbc_call}
   218→link:https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.secrets.versions/add[secrets.versions.add] with appropriate base64 payload
   219→... {rbbc_store}
   220→`«SECRET_VERSION»` from response `.name` field
   221→.. NOTE: api and read_api secrets store the same token value. Webhook secret is auto-generated UUID. The GitLab project access token must have both `api` and `read_api` scopes — CB v2 verifies `read_api` independently.
   222→
   223→//axhos_step
   224→. *Grant Cloud Build service agent Secret Manager access (all 3 secrets)*:
   225→.. Construct service agent: `service-«PROJECT_NUMBER»@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
   226→.. For each of the 3 secrets:
   227→... {rbbc_call}
   228→link:https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.secrets/getIamPolicy[secrets.getIamPolicy] — must succeed (HTTP 200) before modification. Die on failure; never fall back to empty policy.
   229→... Add Cloud Build service agent to `roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor` in the existing policy (read-modify-write)
   230→... {rbbc_call}
   231→link:https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.secrets/setIamPolicy[secrets.setIamPolicy] with the updated policy
   232→.. {rbbc_require}
   233→all IAM bindings successful — existing bindings preserved
   234→
   235→//axhos_step
   236→. *Grant Cloud Build service agent connection admin*:
   237→.. {rbbc_call}
   238→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/setIamPolicy[projects.setIamPolicy] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
   239→.. Add `serviceAccount:service-«PROJECT_NUMBER»@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com` with `roles/cloudbuild.connectionAdmin`
   240→.. {rbbc_require}
   241→IAM binding successful
   242→
   243→//axhos_step
   244→. *Create CB v2 GitLab Connection*:
   245→.. Construct connection name: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»«RBGC_CBV2_CONNECTION_SUFFIX»` (suffix is `-gitlab`)
   246→.. {rbbc_call}
   247→link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v2/projects.locations.connections/create[connections.create] with:
   248→... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}`
   249→... connectionId: `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-gitlab`
   250→... body:
   251→+
   252→----
   253→{
   254→  "gitlabConfig": {
   255→    "authorizerCredential": {
   256→      "userTokenSecretVersion": "«API_TOKEN_SECRET_VERSION»"
   257→    },
   258→    "readAuthorizerCredential": {
   259→      "userTokenSecretVersion": "«READ_TOKEN_SECRET_VERSION»"
   260→    },
   261→    "webhookSecretSecretVersion": "«WEBHOOK_SECRET_VERSION»"
   262→  }
   263→}
   264→----
   265→.. {rbbc_await}
   266→returned {gcp_lro}
   267→.. {rbbc_store}
   268→`«CBV2_CONNECTION_NAME»` as `rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-gitlab`
   269→.. {rbbc_poll}
   270→link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v2/projects.locations.connections/get[connections.get] until `installationState.stage` reaches `COMPLETE`
   271→.. *Retry with backoff on IAM propagation failure*:
   272→The connection creation LRO serves as the IAM propagation gate (see RBSCIP).
   273→If the Cloud Build Service Agent cannot yet access the secrets (grant not propagated),
   274→the LRO fails.  On failure:
   275→... Delete the zombie connection resource via
   276→link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v2/projects.locations.connections/delete[connections.delete]
   277→(a failed LRO leaves a non-functional connection that blocks re-creation with the same name)
   278→... Wait per backoff profile (initial 3s, 2x multiplier, cap 20s, deadline 420s)
   279→... Retry `connections.create` from the top of this step
   280→.. {rbbc_require}
   281→connection is COMPLETE or {rbbc_warn} diagnostic checklist ({rbbc_fatal} after):
   282→... GitLab project access token has both `api` and `read_api` scopes
   283→... Token is a project access token (not personal)
   284→... RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL points to correct GitLab project
   285→
   286→//axhos_step
   287→. *Create CB v2 Repository*:
   288→.. {rbbc_call}
   289→link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v2/projects.locations.connections.repositories/create[repositories.create] with:
   290→... parent: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}/connections/«CBV2_CONNECTION_NAME»`
   291→... repositoryId: `«RBGC_CBV2_REPOSITORY_ID»` (constant, currently `rubric`)
   292→... body: `{"remoteUri": "«RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL»"}`
   293→.. {rbbc_await}
   294→returned {gcp_lro}
   295→.. {rbbc_require}
   296→repository created successfully
   297→
   298→//axhoo_output_of_type
   299→{rbtgi_depot}
   300→. *Display Depot Configuration*:
   301→.. {rbbc_show}
   302→depot creation successful
   303→.. {rbbc_show}
   304→required RBRR configuration values:
   305→... `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
   306→... `RBRR_GCP_REGION={rbrr_gcp_region}`
   307→... `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY=«GAR_REPOSITORY»`
   308→... `RBRR_CBV2_CONNECTION_NAME=«CBV2_CONNECTION_NAME»`
   309→... `RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL=«WORKER_POOL_NAME»`
   310→.. {rbbc_show}
   311→Mason service account: `«MASON_EMAIL»`
   312→.. {rbbc_show}
   313→CB v2 connection `«CBV2_CONNECTION_NAME»` active with GitLab
   314→.. {rbbc_show}
   315→GitLab token stored in Secret Manager (3 secrets: api, read_api, webhook)
   316→
   317→//axhoo_output_of_type
   318→{rbtgi_depot}
   319→. *Update Depot Tracking*:
   320→.. Execute
   321→{rbtoe_depot_list_update}
   322→to refresh depot project tracking
   323→
   324→//axhoc_completion
   325→Successful execution creates depot infrastructure with configured build bucket, container repository, Mason service account, private worker pool, CB v2 GitLab connection (COMPLETE), and CB v2 repository linking to the
   326→{rbtgr_rubric_repo}.
   327→Proceed to
   328→{rbtgo_governor_reset}
   329→to create the
   330→{rbtr_governor}
   331→for this depot.
   332→

1→//axhob_operation
     2→{rbtgo_depot_destroy}
     3→
     4→The
     5→{rbtr_payor}
     6→destroys an
     7→{rbtgi_depot}
     8→and all contained resources:
     9→
    10→//axhop_parameter_from_type
    11→{rbtgi_depot_project}
    12→. *Safety Confirmation*:
    13→.. If `RBGP_CONFIRM_DESTROY` environment variable is set:
    14→... {rbbc_require}
    15→value matches the depot project ID argument
    16→... Proceed without interactive confirmation
    17→.. Otherwise:
    18→... Display comprehensive destruction warnings
    19→... {rbbc_require}
    20→operator confirmation with exact depot project ID (interactive)
    21→
    22→//axhos_step
    23→. *Authenticate as
    24→{rbtr_payor}*:
    25→.. Execute
    26→{rbtoe_payor_authenticate}
    27→.. {rbbc_require}
    28→authentication successful
    29→
    30→//axhos_step
    31→. *Validate Target Depot*:
    32→.. {rbbc_require}
    33→depot project ID provided as first argument
    34→.. {rbbc_store}
    35→`«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` from argument
    36→.. {rbbc_call}
    37→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] for `«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    38→.. {rbbc_require}
    39→project exists and state is `ACTIVE`
    40→.. {rbbc_fatal}
    41→if state is already `DELETE_REQUESTED`: "Project already marked for deletion"
    42→
    43→//axhos_step
    44→. *Check for and remove
    45→{gcp_lien_s}*:
    46→.. {rbbc_call}
    47→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v1/liens/list[liens.list] to list existing liens on resource: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    48→.. {rbbc_store}
    49→`«LIEN_LIST»` from response `.liens[]`
    50→.. For each lien in `«LIEN_LIST»`:
    51→... {rbbc_call}
    52→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v1/liens/delete[liens.delete] to delete lien using `.name` field
    53→... {rbbc_require}
    54→HTTP 200 response for each lien deletion
    55→.. {rbbc_require}
    56→all liens removed before proceeding
    57→
    58→//axhos_step
    59→. *Unlink billing account*:
    60→.. {rbbc_call}
    61→link:https://cloud.google.com/billing/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects/updateBillingInfo[cloudbilling.projects.updateBillingInfo] with body `{"billingAccountName":""}` for `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    62→.. {rbbc_require}
    63→billing unlink successful (HTTP 200)
    64→.. Note: Unlinking BEFORE deletion releases billing quota immediately; deletion without unlink leaves quota consumed for 30-day retention period
    65→
    66→//axhos_step
    67→. *Delete Private Worker Pool*:
    68→.. Construct pool resource name: `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}/workerPools/rbw-«INPUT_DEPOT_NAME»-pool`
    69→.. {rbbc_call}
    70→link:https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.workerPools/delete[workerPools.delete] for the pool resource name
    71→.. Treat HTTP 404 as success (pool already gone — tolerated)
    72→.. {rbbc_require}
    73→HTTP 200 or 404 response; any other status is {rbbc_fatal}
    74→
    75→//axhos_step
    76→. *Initiate depot deletion*:
    77→.. {rbbc_submit}
    78→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/delete[projects.delete] to execute project deletion for `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    79→.. {rbbc_store}
    80→`«DELETE_OPERATION»` from response if Long Running Operation returned
    81→.. {rbbc_await}
    82→`«DELETE_OPERATION»` if applicable
    83→.. {rbbc_require}
    84→deletion initiated successfully
    85→
    86→//axhos_step
    87→. *Verify deletion transition*:
    88→.. {rbbc_call}
    89→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/get[projects.get] to query project state for `projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»`
    90→.. {rbbc_poll}
    91→project state with 5-second intervals for up to 60 seconds
    92→.. {rbbc_require}
    93→project state equals `DELETE_REQUESTED`
    94→
    95→//axhoc_completion
    96→Successful execution destroys depot infrastructure.
    97→
    98→NOTE: Depot deletion automatically removes all infrastructure created by
    99→{rbtgo_depot_create}:
   100→the
   101→{rbtr_mason}
   102→service account,
   103→{rbtgi_build_bucket},
   104→{rbtgi_repository},
   105→and any
   106→{rbtr_governor},
   107→{rbtr_director},
   108→or
   109→{rbtr_retriever}
   110→service accounts created within the depot.
   111→The private worker pool is explicitly deleted before project deletion.
   112→
   113→NOTE: Project enters 30-day retention period in
   114→{gcp_delete_requested}
   115→state.
   116→Use
   117→{rbbc_call}
   118→link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/undelete[projects.undelete] to recover project if needed within retention window.
   119→

content.text: Now let me look at the actual depot_create and depot_destroy function implementations in detail to verify they match the criteria.

556→rbgp_depot_create() {
   557→  zrbgp_sentinel
   558→
   559→  local -r z_depot_name="${1:-}"
   560→  local -r z_region="${RBRR_GCP_REGION}"
   561→
   562→  buc_doc_brief "Create new depot infrastructure following RBAGS specification"
   563→  buc_doc_param "depot_name" "Depot name (lowercase/numbers/hyphens, max ${RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX} chars)"
   564→  buc_doc_shown || return 0
   565→
   566→  buc_step 'Validate input parameters'
   567→  test -n "${z_depot_name}" || buc_die "Depot name required as first argument"
   568→  
   569→  if ! printf '%s' "${z_depot_name}" | grep -qE '^[a-z0-9-]+$'; then
   570→    buc_die "Depot name must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens"
   571→  fi
   572→  
   573→  if [ "${#z_depot_name}" -gt "${RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX}" ]; then
   574→    buc_die "Depot name must be ${RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_NAME_MAX} characters or less"
   575→  fi
   576→
   577→  buc_step 'Validate Rubric Repo URL (GitLab required)'
   578→  local -r z_rubric_repo_url="${RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL:-}"
   579→  if test -z "${z_rubric_repo_url}"; then
   580→    buc_warn "RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL is not set in rbrr.env"
   581→    buc_info "Set up a GitLab rubric repo first:"
   582→    buc_next "${RBZ_GITLAB_SETUP}"
   583→    buc_die "Cannot proceed without GitLab rubric repo"
   584→  fi
   585→
   586→  # Validate GitLab URL — CB v2 gitlabConfig requires gitlab.com
   587→  case "${z_rubric_repo_url}" in
   588→    https://gitlab.com/*) ;;
   589→    *)
   590→      buc_warn "RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL is not a gitlab.com URL"
   591→      buc_info "Only GitLab is supported for CB v2 connections."
   592→      buc_info "Set up a GitLab rubric repo:"
   593→      buc_next "${RBZ_GITLAB_SETUP}"
   594→      buc_die "Cannot proceed without GitLab rubric repo"
   595→      ;;
   596→  esac
   597→
   598→  buc_step 'Read GitLab project access token from stdin'
   599→  buc_info "Rubric repo: ${z_rubric_repo_url}"
   600→  local z_gitlab_token=""
   601→  buc_info "Paste GitLab project access token:"
   602→  read -r z_gitlab_token || buc_die "Failed to read token from stdin"
   603→  if test -z "${z_gitlab_token}"; then
   604→    buc_info "Need a token? Run the setup guide:"
   605→    buc_next "${RBZ_GITLAB_SETUP}"
   606→    buc_die "Token is empty"
   607→  fi
   608→  buc_log_args "GitLab project access token read from stdin"
   609→
   610→  # Construct authenticated URL for validation (token must not persist beyond this check)
   611→  # Bash builtin substitution: https://gitlab.com/... → https://oauth2:TOKEN@gitlab.com/...
   612→  local z_auth_url="https://oauth2:${z_gitlab_token}@${z_rubric_repo_url#https://}"
   613→
   614→  local -r z_lsremote_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_lsremote_stderr.txt"
   615→  git ls-remote "${z_auth_url}" HEAD >/dev/null 2>"${z_lsremote_stderr}" \
   616→    || buc_die "Rubric repo URL unreachable or token invalid — see ${z_lsremote_stderr}"
   617→  unset z_auth_url
   618→  buc_log_args "Rubric repo URL validated"
   619→
   620→  # Validate region exists in Artifact Registry locations
   621→  buc_log_args 'Validating region exists in Artifact Registry locations'
   622→  local z_token
   623→  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor for region validation"
   624→
   625→  local -r z_locations_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/projects/${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}/locations"
   626→  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_locations_url}" "${z_token}" "region_validation"
   627→  rbgu_http_require_ok "Validate region" "region_validation"
   628→  
   629→  local z_valid_regions
   630→  z_valid_regions=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "region_validation" '.locations[].locationId' | tr '\n' ' ') || buc_die "Failed to parse region list"
   631→  
   632→  if ! printf '%s' "${z_valid_regions}" | grep -qw "${z_region}"; then
   633→    buc_die "Invalid region. Valid regions: ${z_valid_regions}"
   634→  fi
   635→
   636→  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
   637→  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"
   638→  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set"
   639→  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID is not set"
   640→  
   641→  local z_token
   642→  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"
   643→
   644→  buc_step 'Generate depot project ID'
   645→  local z_timestamp
   646→  z_timestamp=$(date "${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT}") || buc_die "Failed to generate timestamp"
   647→  local -r z_depot_project_id="${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-${z_depot_name}-${z_timestamp}"
   648→  
   649→  if [ "${#z_depot_project_id}" -gt 30 ]; then
   650→    buc_die "Generated project ID too long (${#z_depot_project_id} > 30): ${z_depot_project_id}"
   651→  fi
   652→  
   653→  buc_log_args "Generated depot project ID: ${z_depot_project_id}"
   654→
   655→  buc_step 'Create depot project'
   656→  local -r z_create_project_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_create_project.json"
   657→
   658→  # OAuth users create projects without parent (per MPCR)
   659→  jq -n \
   660→    --arg projectId "${z_depot_project_id}" \
   661→    --arg displayName "RB Depot ${z_depot_name}" \
   662→    '{
   663→      projectId: $projectId,
   664→      displayName: $displayName
   665→    }' > "${z_create_project_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build project creation body"
   666→
   667→  local -r z_create_project_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects"
   668→  rbgu_http_json_lro_ok \
   669→    "Create depot project" \
   670→    "${z_token}" \
   671→    "${z_create_project_url}" \
   672→    "depot_project_create" \
   673→    "${z_create_project_body}" \
   674→    ".name" \
   675→    "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}" \
   676→    "operations/" \
   677→    "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" \
   678→    "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
   679→
   680→  buc_step 'Link billing account'
   681→  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_link.json"
   682→  jq -n \
   683→    --arg billingAccountName "billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}" \
   684→    '{
   685→      billingAccountName: $billingAccountName
   686→    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing link body"
   687→
   688→  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/billingInfo"
   689→  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_billing_link" "${z_billing_body}"
   690→  rbgu_http_require_ok "Link billing account" "depot_billing_link"
   691→
   692→  buc_step 'Get depot project number'
   693→  local -r z_project_info_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}"
   694→  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_project_info"
   695→  rbgu_http_require_ok "Get project info" "depot_project_info"
   696→  
   697→  local z_project_number

content.content:
# Paddock: rbw-e2e-cbv2-provenance

## Context

End-to-end verification of the Cloud Build v2 trigger pipeline with SLSA v1.0
provenance. This heat validates the infrastructure built in ₣Ai
(gcb-trigger-migration-tier3).

### System Architecture (built by ₣Ai)

**Rubric architecture:** Per-vessel `cloudbuild.json` committed to main repo,
synced to a shared rubric repo (`RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL`) by the inscribe command.
Cloud Build sees only the rubric repo via CB v2 connection — the main repo is
never exposed to Google. Zero custom substitution overrides; all values baked
at inscribe time.

**CB v2 connections** (replaced Developer Connect): Fully programmatic setup via
REST API. Uses GitLab `gitlabConfig` with project access token (3 secrets in
Secret Manager). No browser OAuth consent flow. Connection + repository created
during `depot_create`. GitLab chosen over GitHub for repository-scoped PAT
security (see ₣Ai trophy, `rbgm_gitlab_setup()`).

**Build pipeline (10 steps, stitched by `zrbf_stitch_build_json`):**
1. derive-tag-base (gcloud)
2. get-docker-token (gcloud)
3. docker-login-gar (docker)
4. qemu-binfmt (docker)
5. build-and-export (docker) — OCI Layout Bridge Phase 1
6. push-with-crane (alpine) — OCI Layout Bridge Phase 2
7. **split-oci-platform (skopeo)** — extracts linux/amd64 from multi-platform layout
8. sbom-and-summary (docker) — Syft scans single-platform layout
9. assemble-metadata (alpine)
10. build-and-push-metadata (docker)

**Skopeo split (₢AiABE):** Step 07b uses skopeo to extract a single-platform
OCI layout from the multi-platform archive so Syft can generate SBOMs
(workaround for anchore/syft#1545). SBOM output is per-platform:
`sbom.linux_amd64.spdx.json`.

**Key regime variables:**
- `RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL` — plain HTTPS URL to rubric repo (no credentials)
- `RBRR_CBV2_CONNECTION_NAME` — CB v2 connection identifier
- `RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF` — skopeo image pin (needs refresh on depot create)
- PAT in Secret Manager (3 secrets: `RBGC_CBV2_API_TOKEN_SECRET_NAME`,
  `RBGC_CBV2_READ_TOKEN_SECRET_NAME`, `RBGC_CBV2_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME`)

### Decision: Private Pool Always (2026-03-03)

**Burn the default-pool bridge.** Every depot gets a private pool. No conditional
path in stitch, no "optional" regime variable, no two configurations to debug.

**Rationale:** Private pools are the prerequisite for higher security tiers.
VPC Service Controls (data exfiltration perimeter) **only works with private pools**.
NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (build worker network isolation) **requires private pools**.
The roadmap (RBSCB) treats private pools as current posture and both hardening
tiers as future stages — every depot must be on private pools to keep that path open.

Additionally, pricing is essentially identical (~$0.003/vCPU-min, 1.0-1.13x ratio),
and the conditional in `zrbf_stitch_build_json` created two code paths, two depot
configurations, two things to debug. One path, well-tested, is better.

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile the full assessment.

---

## Pace ₢AlAAB Acceptance Criteria Assessment

Here is the complete finding for each criterion:

---

### Criterion 1: No default-pool code path remains in stitch or depot constants

**PASS.**

`rbf_Foundry.sh` `zrbf_stitch_build_json()` (lines 237-283):
- No conditional on `RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL` — it is used unconditionally at line 255
- No `machineType` in options; the only options emitted are `logging` and `pool`
- No `E2_HIGHCPU_8`, `UNSPECIFIED`, or any default-pool enum anywhere in the stitch path

`rbgc_Constants.sh` lines 163-164 contain only:
```
readonly RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX="-pool"
readonly RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS="/workerPools"
```
No `RBGC_WORKER_POOL_ID` constant and no default-pool path.

`rbgd_DepotConstants.sh` contains no worker pool or quota-check references at all — clean.

The only mentions of "default-pool" in the Tools/rbw tree are inside comment text in `rbrr.validator.sh` (lines 28, 30, 32), which are correct: they explain *why* the CE format is being enforced — to reject default-pool enums.

---

### Criterion 2: `RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL` is required (regime validator rejects empty)

**PASS.**

`rbrr.validator.sh` lines 33-35:
```bash
# Worker pool is required (private pool always — no default pool path)
test -n "${RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL:-}" || buc_die "RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL not set (private pool is required)"
[[ "${RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL}" =~ ^projects/[^/]+/locations/[^/]+/workerPools/[^/]+$ ]] \
  || buc_die "RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL must match projects/{P}/locations/{L}/workerPools/{W}: ..."
```

`rbrr_regime.sh` enrollment at line 66:
```bash
buv_string_enroll  RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL  1  512  "Private Pool resource name (required)"
```
The minimum length of 1 means `buv_vet` will also reject empty. Both layers enforce it.

---

### Criterion 3: `RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE` default is `e2-standard-2`, validated as CE type

**PASS WITH ONE OBSERVATION.**

`.rbk/rbrr.env` line 45:
```bash
RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-standard-2          # Compute Engine machine type for private pool workerConfig
```
Default is `e2-standard-2`. Correct.

`rbrr.validator.sh` lines 27-30:
```bash
# Machine type must be Compute Engine format (lowercase with hyphens) for private pool workerConfig
# Reject default-pool enum format (uppercase with underscores like UNSPECIFIED, E2_HIGHCPU_8)
[[ "${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]+$ ]] \
  || buc_die "RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE must be Compute Engine format (e.g., e2-standard-2), not default-pool enum: '${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}'"
```
CE format validated. Correctly rejects enum format.

**Observation:** The `rbrr_regime.sh` enrollment (`buv_string_enroll RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE 3 64`) validates length 3-64 but does not validate CE format — that is handled by `rbrr.validator.sh`. This is consistent by design since the validator is a separate sourced script (line 35 of `rbgd_DepotConstants.sh` sources `rbrr.validator.sh`). The CE-format regex lives in the right place.

---

### Criterion 4: Stitch emits only `pool.name`, never `machineType` in options

**PASS.**

`rbf_Foundry.sh` lines 277-281, the options block in the jq composition:
```json
options: {
  logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
  pool: { name: $zjq_pool }
},
```
No `machineType` field anywhere in the stitch output. The jq query `grep machineType` across the full foundry file returned zero hits.

---

### Criterion 5: `depot_create` creates pool using `RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE`, `depot_destroy` deletes pool

**PASS.**

`rbgp_Payor.sh` `rbgp_depot_create()` lines 708-733:
```bash
buc_step 'Create private worker pool'
local -r z_pool_id="rbw-${z_depot_name}${RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX}"
...
jq -n --arg machineType "${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}" \
  '{
    privatePoolV1Config: {
      workerConfig: {
        machineType: $machineType
      }
    }
  }' > "${z_pool_create_body}"
...
rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_pool_create_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_pool_create" "${z_pool_create_body}"
```
Pool is created unconditionally (no conditional on whether pool is set), using `RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE`. HTTP 409 treated as success (idempotent). Pool resource path is assembled and logged.

`rbgp_depot_destroy()` lines 1261-1270:
```bash
buc_step 'Delete worker pool (if exists)'
local -r z_pool_id="rbw-${z_depot_name}${RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX}"
local -r z_pool_del_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}.../${z_pool_id}"
rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_pool_del_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_pool"
case "${z_pool_del_code}" in
  200|204|404) buc_log_args "Worker pool ${z_pool_id} cleanup: HTTP ${z_pool_del_code}" ;;
  *) buc_warn "Worker pool cleanup failed: HTTP ${z_pool_del_code} — proceeding" ;;
esac
```
Pool is deleted before project deletion. HTTP 404 accepted as success (pool already gone — tolerated). This matches the spec in `RBSDD-depot_destroy.adoc` exactly.

---

### Criterion 6: Decision chain documented in spec (not just paddock)

**PASS WITH CAVEAT.**

`RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` lines 2152-2165:
```
[[rbrr_gcb_worker_pool]]
...
Builds always use a private pool; the default shared pool is not supported.
`options.machineType` is omitted from the build JSON — machine type is a property of the pool resource.

NOTE: *Private pool is always required.*
Private pools are a prerequisite for VPC Service Controls and `NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS` hardening tiers.
See heat ₣Al paddock for full decision rationale.
```

The paddock (`jjx_paddock Al`) contains the full decision section titled "Decision: Private Pool Always (2026-03-03)" with complete rationale covering security prerequisites, pricing comparison, and complexity reduction.

**Caveat:** The spec itself provides the normative statement (private pool always required, VPC-SC prerequisite) and defers to the paddock for the full decision chain. The spec does *not* reproduce the full rationale inline — it points to the paddock. Whether this satisfies "documented in spec" depends on your intent. If the spec must be self-contained, there is a gap. If "documented in spec with paddock reference" is acceptable, it passes.

There is also a stale passage in `RBSQB-quota_build.adoc` lines 88-91:
```
NOTE: If the desired concurrency exceeds what the default pool CPU quota permits even after machine type adjustment, a GCB private worker pool with explicit concurrency settings is an alternative. This would require additional regime configuration beyond the scope of this procedure.
```
This implies private pools are *optional*. Since private pools are now always required, this note is technically misleading. It does not break functionality but it contradicts the "private pool always" policy.

---

### Criterion 7: Build passes

**CANNOT VERIFY** — this is a runtime check. No Rust or compiled code is involved in these files; they are bash scripts. The only static check possible is reading the files, which has been done above and shows no obvious syntax problems (no unclosed brackets, correct jq usage, etc.). To verify build passes definitively, `tt/rbw-qa.QualifyAll.sh` or the specific test tabtarget must be run.

---

## Summary Table

| Criterion | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| 1. No default-pool code path in stitch/constants | PASS | Zero conditional paths; only dead mentions in validator comments |
| 2. `RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL` required | PASS | Both validator and buv enrollment enforce non-empty |
| 3. `RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE` default `e2-standard-2`, CE validated | PASS | Default set; CE regex in validator |
| 4. Stitch emits `pool.name` only, no `machineType` in options | PASS | Confirmed in jq composition |
| 5. depot_create creates pool, depot_destroy deletes pool | PASS | Both unconditional; 409/404 idempotent |
| 6. Decision chain in spec | PARTIAL | Normative text in RBS0; full rationale in paddock only; stale RBSQB note contradicts |
| 7. Build passes | NOT VERIFIED | Requires runtime execution |

The two items that deserve attention before closing:

1. **RBSQB-quota_build.adoc lines 88-91**: Refers to private pools as an "alternative" — stale now that private pools are mandatory. Cosmetic but contradictory.
2. **Criterion 6 interpretation**: The spec says "See heat ₣Al paddock for full decision rationale" rather than embedding the rationale. If the acceptance criterion requires rationale in the spec document itself (not just a pointer to the paddock), this is incomplete.
